input.background: For the illustrative example, we have established that when \bar{x}=173 (pounds), P=0.27. This P-value indicates that 173 is not significantly different from 170 at conventional levels of \alpha (retain H_0). In contrast, when \bar{x}=185, P=0.0013, thus providing highly significant evidence against H_0 (reject H_0).
input.question: When \bar{x}=173, is the P-value significant at conventional levels of \alpha?

output.answer: The P-value of 0.27 indicates that 173 is not significantly different from 170 at conventional levels of \alpha (retain H_0).

P_value.description: P-values corresponding to different sample mean values.

alpha.description: Significance level used to determine the significance of the P-value.
